tables.title: Delirium vs. Dementia
rows: Often reversible once cause is treated
Infection, medication effects, hypoxia, electrolyte disturbance, dehydration, urinary retention/constipation, pain
Neurodegenerative disease (e.g., Alzheimer's), vascular changes, other chronic processes
Assume delirium for any new/acute confusion until proven otherwise
Known, stable baseline — sudden worsening should prompt a delirium workup, not be assumed as dementia progression

tables.title: Atypical Geriatric Presentations of Common Conditions
rows: Confusion, weakness, or dyspnea alone ('silent MI')
